request.question: Sandra Dallas and Dee Brown, are of which nationality?

sentences: Daniel N. Paul, {'1': ", '2': ", '3': ", '4': "} , (born 1938) is a Mi'kmaq Elder, author, columnist, and human rights activist.
Paul is perhaps best known as the author of the book "We Were Not the Savages".
Paul asserts that this book is the first such history ever written by a First Nation citizen.
The book is seen as an important contribution to the North American Indian movement.

One writer stated, "It’s a Canadian version of Dee Brown’s best seller Bury My Heart at Wounded Knee and, as such, served a valuable purpose in raising public consciousness about Mi’kmaq history, identity, and culture."
James Turner Brewer (born December 3, 1951) is a retired American National Basketball Association (NBA) player.
Brewer was the first notable player to come out of Proviso East High School, which has one of the most successful high school basketball programs in Illinois.
In 1969, Brewer, playing center, led his team to the first of four state championships.
Brewer was followed at Proviso East by other future NBA players, notably Doc Rivers, Michael Finley, Dee Brown, and Shannon Brown.
The 6'9" 210 pound forward then attended the University of Minnesota, where he was a teammate of future Baseball Hall of Famer Dave Winfield.

Brewer played in the 1972 Summer Olympics, including the United States' controversial loss to the Soviet Union, before being drafted by the Cleveland Cavaliers in the first round (2nd pick) of the 1973 NBA draft.
When Brewer scored a basket at a Cavaliers home game, the public address announcer would declare, "Two for the Brew!"
Brewer played nine seasons in the NBA from 1973 to 1982.
Then he played with Pallacanestro Cantù in Italian Serie A along with players as Pierluigi Marzorati and Antonello Riva with coach Giancarlo Primo.
He won a Euroleague and was an Intercontinental Cup finalist.
Jubilee Trail is a novel written by Gwen Bristow, copyrighted in 1950.
It follows the adventures of two strong women in the mid-19th century as they travel across the United States to the then-Mexican territory of California.
The novel is still in print, with forewords included by Nancy E. Turner and Sandra Dallas.
Sandra Dallas is an award-winning American author of 13 adult novels, 2 children's novels, and 7 non-fiction books.
Previously, she was an ex-reporter and bureau chief for "BusinessWeek" magazine for the Denver region.

Bury My Heart at Wounded Knee: An Indian History of the American West is a 1970 book by American writer Dee Brown that covers the history of Native Americans in the American West in the late nineteenth century.
The book expresses details of the history of American expansionism from a point of view that is critical of its effects on the Native Americans.
Brown describes Native Americans' displacement through forced relocations and years of warfare waged by the United States federal government.
The government's dealings are portrayed as a continuing effort to destroy the culture, religion, and way of life of Native American peoples.
Helen Hunt Jackson's "A Century of Dishonor" is often considered a nineteenth-century precursor to Dee Brown's writing.
The Rocky Mountain Quilt Museum (RMQM) is a cultural and historical quilt museum in Golden, Colorado.
The permanent collection contains over 550 quilts dating back to the 19th century.
The museum is also home to the Sandra Dallas Library and a gift shop.
The 2000–01 NBA season was the 12th season for the Orlando Magic in the National Basketball Association.
During the offseason, the Magic acquired Tracy McGrady from the Toronto Raptors, and All-Star forward Grant Hill from the Detroit Pistons, while signing free agents Troy Hudson and Dee Brown.
However, Hill re-injured his ankle from the previous season after playing just four games, as the Magic struggled losing 11 of their first 16 games in November.
Meanwhile, Brown only appeared in just seven games due to a left quadricep tendon.

Despite losing both Hill and Brown, the Magic posted a nine-game winning streak in February, and returned to the playoffs after a one-year absence, finishing fourth in the Atlantic Division with a 43–39 record.
McGrady, who averaged 26.8 points per game was named Most Improved Player of the Year, and was selected for the 2001 NBA All-Star Game.
Top draft pick Mike Miller was named Rookie of the Year averaging 11.9 points per game.
Kevin "Bizness" Butler is an American streetball player known for his exceptional dunking ability.
He is also known for his appearances on the ESPN television show "City Slam".
Butler is from Oakland, California and is 6 foot 2 inches tall.
Butler played college basketball for 4 years for the University of California - Riverside (UCR), and, like his streetball moniker, ""Bizness"", alludes, graduated in 2004 with a degree in business.
Butler also plays for the streetball crew "YPA".
Butler competed in the slam dunk contest of City Slam in both the 2005 and 2006 seasons.
During the 2005 season, Butler was one of three contestants to reach the 60-inch barrier in the high-jump portion of the competition.
In the 2005 finals in Chicago, Butler placed fourth, losing to eventual champ, Chris "Skywalker" Lowery.
In the 2006 season, Butler was the Oakland Regional winner.
In 2005, Butler played in the inaugural season for the then San Francisco Pilots (later renamed the San Francisco Rumble) of the reborn American Basketball Association.

In 2007, Butler, along with other past winners and finalists from City Slam, assisted former NBA player Dee Brown during his "2007 Summer Slam Camp", which was described as the "world’s first slam dunk workout camp".
Dorris Alexander "Dee" Brown (February 29, 1908 – December 12, 2002) was an American novelist, historian, and librarian.
His most famous work, "Bury My Heart at Wounded Knee" (1970) details the history of American expansionism from the point of view of the Native Americans.
The 1999–2000 NBA season was the Raptors' fifth season in the National Basketball Association.
During the offseason, the Raptors acquired Antonio Davis from the Indiana Pacers, while signing free agents Muggsy Bogues and three-point specialist Dell Curry.
In their first full season playing at the Air Canada Centre, the Raptors got better as the season progressed.
Second-year star Vince Carter led the team in scoring, averaging 25.7 points per game.
He also won the Slam Dunk Contest in Oakland and was selected for the 2000 NBA All-Star Game.
The Raptors improved and qualified for their first playoff appearance with a 45–37 record, finishing third in the Central Division.
However, in the postseason, they were swept in the first round by the New York Knicks in three straight games.

Following the season, Tracy McGrady was traded to the Orlando Magic, Doug Christie was dealt to the Sacramento Kings, Dee Brown signed as a free agent with the Magic, and head coach Butch Carter was fired.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Sandra Dallas and Dee Brown, are of which nationality?
hop1_docs: Sandra Bond Chapman | Sandra Bond Chapman is a cognitive neuroscientist, founder and chief director of the Center for BrainHealth, Dee Wyly Distinguished Professor in Brain Health, and a professor in the School of Behavioral and Brain Sciences at The University of Texas at Dallas.

Sandra Dee | Sandra Dee (born Alexandra Zuck; April 23, 1942 – February 20, 2005) was an American actress.  Dee began her career as a child model, working in commercials before transitioning to film in her teenage years.  Best known for her portrayal of ingénues, Dee earned a Golden Globe Award as one of the year's most promising newcomers for her performance in Robert Wise's "Until They Sail" (1958).  She became a teenage star for her subsequent performances in "Imitation of Life" and "Gidget" (both 1959), which made her a household name.

Sandra Dee Robinson | Sandra Dee Robinson, also sometimes credited as Sandra Robinson, was born March 23, 1967 in Pittsburgh, PA.  She is an actress and founder of the media training and consulting firm Charisma On Camera.  She was Miss Pennsylvania USA 1985.

The Restless Years (film) | The Restless Years is a 1958 American CinemaScope drama film directed by Helmut Käutner starring John Saxon, Sandra Dee, Teresa Wright, James Whitmore and Luana Patten.  It was Sandra Dee's first leading role.

Dee Brown (basketball, born 1968) | DeCovan Kadell "Dee" Brown (born November 29, 1968) is an American retired professional basketball player who spent twelve seasons (1990–2002) in the National Basketball Association (NBA), playing for the Boston Celtics, Toronto Raptors, and Orlando Magic.

Dee Brown (basketball, born 1984) | Daniel "Dee" Brown (born August 17, 1984) is a retired American professional basketball player and current college coach.  Brown played at the University of Illinois from 2002 to 2006, receiving numerous awards and accolades, including the 2005 Sporting News National Player of the Year.  Brown was selected in the second round of the 2006 NBA Draft by the Utah Jazz, and was the starting point guard for 11 consecutive games for the Washington Wizards.  Brown played for several international basketball teams from 2007 to 2015.

Cheryl James | Cheryl James (born March 28, 1966) better known by her stage name Salt, is an American rapper, songwriter and actress.  James is best known as a member of the American rap trio Salt-n-Pepa, which also includes Pepa (Sandra Denton) and Spinderella (Deidra "Dee Dee" Roper).  James starred in "The Salt-n-Pepa Show", a reality TV series focusing on reforming the group which aired on the VH1 network in 2008.
response.summary_1: Evidence that Dee Brown is American: [5] "DeCovan Kadell 'Dee' Brown ... is an American retired professional basketball player" and [6] "Daniel 'Dee' Brown ... is a retired American professional basketball player." Missing/ambiguous for Sandra: none of the passages mention "Sandra Dallas" — they show other Sandras ("Sandra Bond Chapman", "Sandra Dee", "Sandra Dee Robinson") but do not link to "Sandra Dallas." Next hop: retrieve an authoritative biography or page titled "Sandra Dallas" (e.g., search query: "Sandra Dallas biography" or "Sandra Dallas nationality") to confirm her nationality.
hop2_docs: Sandra Dallas | Sandra Dallas is an award-winning American author of 13 adult novels, 2 children's novels, and 7 non-fiction books.  Previously, she was an ex-reporter and bureau chief for "BusinessWeek" magazine for the Denver region.

Sandra Bond Chapman | Sandra Bond Chapman is a cognitive neuroscientist, founder and chief director of the Center for BrainHealth, Dee Wyly Distinguished Professor in Brain Health, and a professor in the School of Behavioral and Brain Sciences at The University of Texas at Dallas.

Dallas Doll | Dallas Doll is an Australian film starring Sandra Bernhard, David Ngoombujarra, Roy Billing, Victoria Longley, Frank Gallacher, Jake Blundell, Rose Byrne and written and directed by Ann Turner.

Sandra St. Victor | Sandra St. Victor is a Dallas-born singer-songwriter most known for her work as lead singer of The Family Stand.  She attended the now famous Booker T. Washington High School for the Performing and Visual Arts, (Arts Magnet) High School studying all styles of music.  Arts Magnet was also stomping grounds for musical colleagues Erykah Badu, Roy Hargrove, Norah Jones and Edie Brickell.  She received scholarships in music to Kansas University & Bishop College in Dallas, Texas, where she continued her studies, until deciding she'd rather learn by doing.  She joined a local band in Dallas, and began touring Texas and Louisiana.  The band was led by jazz guitarist Zachary Breaux.

Sports in Dallas | The City of Dallas and the Dallas metropolitan area is home to teams in six major sports: the Dallas Cowboys (National Football League), Dallas Mavericks (National Basketball Association), Texas Rangers (Major League Baseball), FC Dallas (Major League Soccer), Dallas Stars (National Hockey League) and Dallas Wings (Women's National Basketball Association).

Sandra, Estonia | Sandra is a village in Suure-Jaani Parish, Viljandi County, Estonia.  It has a population of 28 (as of 2009).  Sandra is located in the Soomaa National Park.  Due to large wetland areas being included in its boundaries, Sandra is the largest village by area in Estonia with 173 km² (larger than the area of Tallinn).

Sandra Daškova | Sandra Daškova (born November 14, 1991 in Tallinn) is an Estonian beauty pageant titleholder.  Sandra won the national contest which allowed her to participate in the Miss International 2011.
response.summary_2: Both Sandra Dallas and Dee Brown are American. Passage [1] identifies Sandra Dallas as an "award-winning American author," and the context cites Dee Brown as a retired American professional basketball player.
